name: Ouisie's Black Bean Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 yellow onion, peeled, cored and cut into 6 wedges
4 whole cloves, stuck into one of the onion wedges
1 large clove of garlic, with a toothpick stuck through it so you can remove it easily
3 cups chicken broth, fresh, canned or dehydrated (taste whichever you choose and salt accordingly)
3 carrots, washed but not peeled, and chopped
3 celery stalks and leaves, washed and chopped
3 cloves garlic, peeled and finely chopped
6 tablespoons Sour cream, preferably at room temperature and stirred so it spoons nicely
Fresh cilantro – washed and spun dry
recipeInstructions:
1. The night before, wash the beans thoroughly in a colander and pick out rocks and earth and any malformed specimens. Place them in a bowl with enough fresh, cold water to cover by 3 inches and set aside.
2. The next day, drain the beans and put them in a large soup pot. Cover with cold water and turn the burner on low. As the beans heat, foam will rise to the surface; skim this off with a large cooking spoon and repeat until all foam is gone (do not let the pot boil).
3. Add onion wedges, garlic, and bay leaves. Simmer, stirring occasionally and adding water when the liquid gets low.
4. When the beans are tender (takes from one to three hours), remove onion wedges and garlic. Spoon out half of the beans and puree in a food processor with chicken broth. Return mixture to the pot.
5. Add salt to taste and simmer while preparing vegetables.
6. Toast cumin seeds in a non-oiled saute pan and grind them with a mortar and pestle or electric spice-grinder. Heat oil in the same pan and cook carrots, celery, and garlic until translucent.
7. Add cumin, thyme, chili flakes, and stir. Add vegetables to bean pot, deglaze with a little dry vermouth or water, and add this liquid to beans as well. Check seasoning, simmer another 10 minutes, and soup is ready.
8. Serve in heated bowls so the last bite remains warm. Top each serving with a teaspoon of Sour cream, chopped green onion, cilantro, and serve lime wedge for squeezing into soup.
